result:
Confirmed: single definition of `struct redisObject` at `src/object.h:100`, with `robj`/`kvobj` as typedef aliases of it (`src/object.h:115,118`) — this codebase doesn't have a separate `kvobj` struct; `kvobj` is the same struct used when it carries key/metadata.

## Struct definition — `src/object.h:100-112`

```c
struct redisObject {
    unsigned type:4;
    unsigned encoding:4;
    unsigned refcount : OBJ_REFCOUNT_BITS;   // 23 bits
    unsigned iskvobj : 1;   /* 1 if this struct serves as a kvobj base */
    unsigned metabits :8;   /* only valid when iskvobj is set */
    unsigned lru:LRU_BITS;  /* only valid when iskvobj is set */
    void *ptr;
};
typedef struct redisObject robj;   // object.h:115
typedef struct redisObject kvobj;  // object.h:118
```

## Fields/constants that drive reference-counting behavior

- **`refcount`** (`OBJ_REFCOUNT_BITS` = 23 bits, `object.h:95,103`) — the actual counter. `incrRefCount`/`decrRefCount` mutate this field.
- **`OBJ_SHARED_REFCOUNT`** = `(1<<23)-1` (`object.h:96`) — sentinel value marking a *shared/immutable* object (e.g. the small-integer shared pool). When `refcount == OBJ_SHARED_REFCOUNT`:
  - `incrRefCount` is a no-op (`object.c:633-634`)
  - `decrRefCount` returns immediately without freeing (`object.c:644-645`)
  - Set via `makeObjectShared()` (`object.c:145`)
- **`OBJ_STATIC_REFCOUNT`** = `(1<<23)-2` (`object.h:97`) — marks an object allocated on the stack. `incrRefCount` panics if you try to retain such an object (`object.c:635-636`), since stack objects must never be reference-counted/heap-freed.
- **`OBJ_FIRST_SPECIAL_REFCOUNT`** = `OBJ_STATIC_REFCOUNT` (`object.h:98`) — the boundary: normal increment only happens while `refcount < OBJ_FIRST_SPECIAL_REFCOUNT - 1` (`object.c:630`); at/above that, any refcount not equal to the two special sentinels triggers a "maximum refcount" panic (`object.c:637-638`).
- **`iskvobj`** (1 bit, `object.h:104`) — not a counter itself, but gates free-path behavior on `refcount` reaching 0: when set, `decrRefCount` computes the real allocation pointer via `kvobjGetAllocPtr()` and additionally runs `keyMetaOnFree()` if metadata bits are present (`object.c:663-668`), and it also disables the embedded-string fast-free path (`object.c:655`, requires `!o->iskvobj`).
- **`type` / `encoding`** — not part of the counting logic itself, but consulted when `refcount` hits 0 to pick the correct type-specific free function (`object.c:672-685`) and to decide the embstr fast-free path (`object.c:655`).

### Behavior summary (`incrRefCount`/`decrRefCount`, `object.c:629-689`)
- Normal objects: increment/decrement `refcount` normally; free (`zfree`) when it reaches 0, after calling the type's free handler and handling `iskvobj` bookkeeping.
- Shared objects (`refcount == OBJ_SHARED_REFCOUNT`): refcount is frozen — inc/dec are both no-ops.
- Static/stack objects (`refcount == OBJ_STATIC_REFCOUNT`): retaining (`incrRefCount`) is illegal and panics; presumably such objects are never decremented through this path either.
- Any other refcount at/above `OBJ_FIRST_SPECIAL_REFCOUNT-1` that isn't one of the two sentinels is treated as overflow and panics ("maximum refcount").

Method note: struct located via `workspaceSymbol`/grep (single definition confirmed — only one `redisObject` symbol in the whole workspace), field semantics and refcount logic read directly from `src/object.h:90-186` and `src/object.c:629-689`.
